content.text:
**Posts matching the search criteria (since 2026-07-14, Latest mode):**

**1.**  
- **@handle**: @Project0_Z  
- **Full text**: Off-topic from the meetup but saw the bounty/DAO Discord bot PR in your governance repo — if it needs to decide whether submitted work qualifies for payout, that part's already solved. Verdikta (https://bounties.verdikta.org/ is an open-source AI-jury evaluation layer on Base L2, ~$0.45/eval, could plug into the bot via API instead of building scoring logic from scratch. Happy to share docs.  
- **Date**: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 23:56:22 GMT  
- **Author type**: Builder / project (Bio: “Sintoide | Consciencia + AI | Autor y co-fundador de DePINLA Zeta_v1 ERC-8004 #38306”)  
- **Engagement**: Likes=0, Reposts=0, Quotes=0, Replies=0, Bookmarks=0, Views=53  
- **Direct link**: https://x.com/Project0_Z/status/2077905210949652681

**2.**  
- **@handle**: @verdikta19633  
- **Full text**: AI scams are getting realistic enough to scare normal people, not just companies. If software can act for us, we need clear rules for what it is allowed to do and an audit log (who did what). Would you rather block risky actions by default, or let them run and review later?  
- **Date**: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 07:00:39 GMT  
- **Author type**: Project (Bio: “Decentralized judgment and settlement layer for agent economies.”)  
- **Engagement**: Likes=1, Reposts=0, Quotes=0, Replies=0, Bookmarks=0, Views=38  
- **Direct link**: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2077649597573308734

**3.**  
- **@handle**: @verdikta19633  
- **Full text**: We broke a 0.05 ETH content commission into five sequenced bounties with separate rubrics, deadlines, and escrow — instead of one large payment on faith. Task A is the GitHub Pages playbook. Tasks B, C, D are three Medium case studies, each a different bounty type. Task E pays only after 100 verified views across the full series, gathered 30 days post-publication. Every deliverable requires the hunter to publish under their own account and share publicly before scoring begins. Multi-model jury scores each submission against locked criteria. The creator cannot adjust the rubric after submission arrives. This is what procurement looks like when pre-commitment is enforced at the infrastructure level — not a contract, not a handshake, but verifiable criteria locked on-chain before work starts.  
- **Date**: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 15:00:40 GMT  
- **Author type**: Project (Bio: “Decentralized judgment and settlement layer for agent economies.”)  
- **Engagement**: Likes=1, Reposts=0, Quotes=0, Replies=0, Bookmarks=0, Views=57
